source_clause_title: 15.4 SUSPENSION BY COMPANY FOR CONVENIENCE. (a) Company may suspend all or a portion of the Work to be performed under the Contract Documents at any time for any reason in its sole discretion by giving written notice thereof to Contractor.

entities.name: Company
entities.legal_role: The party with the unilateral right to suspend the Work for convenience and to issue notices affecting performance and termination.
entities.evidence_quote: "Company may suspend all or a portion of the Work to be performed under the Contract Documents at any time for any reason in its sole discretion by giving written notice thereof to Contractor."

entities.name: Contractor
entities.legal_role: The party obligated to perform the Work and to comply with suspension and termination instructions from Company.
entities.evidence_quote: "Contractor agrees to resume performance of the Work promptly upon receipt of notice from Company."

entities.name: Work
entities.legal_role: The subject matter of the contract that may be suspended or resumed by Company and performed by Contractor.
entities.evidence_quote: "Company may suspend all or a portion of the Work to be performed under the Contract Documents..."

entities.name: Notice of Suspension
entities.legal_role: Written communication from Company to Contractor effectuating suspension of all or part of the Work.
entities.evidence_quote: "...by giving written notice thereof to Contractor."

entities.name: Standby Charge
entities.legal_role: A reimbursable cost to Contractor for direct costs of maintaining organization and equipment on standby during suspension.
entities.evidence_quote: "(i) a standby charge, without mark-up or multiplier, sufficient to compensate Contractor for the direct costs attributable to keeping... its organization and the Contractor Equipment committed to the Work on a standby basis..."

entities.entity_type: Reimbursable Cost Component (Demobilization and Remobilization Costs)
entities.name: Demobilization and Remobilization Costs
entities.legal_role: Costs reimbursed to Contractor for necessary and reasonable expenses related to demobilizing and remobilizing labor, equipment, and facilities due to suspension.
entities.evidence_quote: "(ii) all necessary and reasonable costs incurred in connection with demobilization and remobilization of Contractor’s facility and Labor and the Contractor Equipment;"

entities.name: Protection Costs
entities.legal_role: Equitable reimbursement to Contractor for costs of receiving, maintaining, and protecting suspended Work.
entities.evidence_quote: "(iii) an equitable amount to reimburse Contractor for the cost of receiving, maintaining and protecting that portion of Work upon which performance has been suspended..."

entities.name: Change Order Request
entities.legal_role: Contractor’s formal request for adjustment due to suspension, subject to timing and conditions, to seek compensation or schedule relief.
entities.evidence_quote: "Contractor may request a Change Order as a result of a suspension of Work under this Section 15.4 within [*****] after receipt of notice to resume the suspended Work..."

entities.name: Force Majeure Event
entities.legal_role: An event excusing performance delays or suspensions, limiting Contractor’s entitlement to adjustments for suspensions caused by such events.
entities.evidence_quote: "No adjustment to the Guaranteed Completion Date, Contract Price or other terms herein shall be made for any suspension of Work under this Section 15.4 to the extent that performance would have been suspended, delayed or interrupted as a result of any Force Majeure Event..."

entities.entity_type: Third Party Contractual Participants (Subcontractors and Vendors)
entities.name: Subcontractors and Vendors
entities.legal_role: Entities engaged by Contractor whose contracts may be suspended or assigned to Company upon termination.
entities.evidence_quote: "Contractor shall use reasonable commercial efforts to include a suspension for convenience provision with terms similar to the foregoing in all subcontracts and purchase orders."

entities.name: Selective Subcontractors/Vendors
entities.legal_role: Designated subcontractors or vendors whose contracts may be assigned to Company upon termination.
entities.evidence_quote: "...whether by Contractor or Company, Company shall have the additional assignment rights set forth in this subparagraph as to the following Subcontractors or Vendors: [*****] (collectively, “Selective Subcontractors/Vendors”)."

entities.name: Notice of Termination
entities.legal_role: Written communication effectuating termination of the Agreement by either Party under specified conditions.
entities.evidence_quote: "...either Party may deliver a written notice to the other Party stating its intention to terminate this Agreement."

entities.name: Cure Period
entities.legal_role: The time allowed for a breaching party to remedy a default before termination rights arise, which may be waived in certain circumstances.
entities.evidence_quote: "Company may issue a notice of termination without first issuing a cure notice or prior to the expiration of a cure period where waiting for the expiration of the cure period would be futile or where the default is deemed not curable..."

entities.name: Company Event of Default
entities.legal_role: Specific breaches or failures by Company that, if uncured, entitle Contractor to terminate the Agreement.
entities.evidence_quote: "The occurrence and continuation of any of the following events shall constitute an event of default by Company (each, a “Company Event of Default”): (a) a failure by Company to make payment..."

entities.name: Contractor Event of Default
entities.legal_role: Specific breaches or failures by Contractor that may justify termination by Company without cure period.
entities.evidence_quote: "defaults which are not curable include, but are not limited to, the following: (a) A Contractor Event of Default under Section 15.1(a)."

entities.name: Job Site
entities.legal_role: Physical location where Work is performed and from which Contractor must remove equipment and materials upon termination.
entities.evidence_quote: "Contractor shall leave the Job Site and remove from the Job Site all the Contractor Equipment, waste, rubbish and Hazardous Material... as Company may request;"

entities.name: Contractor Equipment
entities.legal_role: Equipment owned or controlled by Contractor used in performance of Work, subject to removal or assignment upon termination or suspension.
entities.evidence_quote: "Contractor shall leave the Job Site and remove from the Job Site all the Contractor Equipment..."

entities.name: Company Data
entities.legal_role: Data in Contractor’s possession subject to retrieval by Company following termination.
entities.evidence_quote: "For [*****] following any termination for cause or convenience, Company will have the right to retrieve any Company Data in Contractor’s possession..."

extractor_notes: This clause extensively details the rights and obligations related to suspension for convenience by Company, including Contractor's duties during suspension, reimbursement rights, and conditions for resumption of work. It also integrates termination provisions, cure periods, and assignment rights upon termination, reflecting a comprehensive risk allocation and operational control framework. The clause explicitly excludes adjustments for suspensions caused by Force Majeure Events and noncompliance by Contractor, emphasizing the importance of clear notice and documentation requirements for claims. source_clause_title: 2.1 RETENTION OF CONTRACTOR. Company hereby engages Contractor, and Contractor hereby agrees to be engaged by Company to perform the Work in accordance with the terms and conditions set forth herein.

relationships.legal_significance: Company engages Contractor to perform the Work under the Contract Documents, establishing the primary contractual relationship.

relationships.legal_significance: Contractor may retain Substantial Subcontractors and Vendors listed on Exhibit J without further approval from Company.

relationships.evidence_quote: Company-provided insurance required pursuant to this Section 9.9 shall: (i) provide a waiver of subrogation in favor of Contractor...

relationships.relationship_type: Duty to Cooperate in Insurance Claims
relationships.legal_significance: Contractor must assist and cooperate fully with Company in adjusting losses and pursuing insurance coverage under Company’s policies, ensuring efficient claims handling.
relationships.evidence_quote: Contractor will assist Company in the adjustment of losses and shall cooperate fully and expeditiously with Company in Company’s pursuit of insurance coverage.

relationships.legal_significance: Contractor is responsible for managing all Contractor Insurance Policies, including payment of deductibles and filing claims, which affects risk management and financial exposure.
relationships.evidence_quote: Contractor shall be responsible for (a) managing and administering all Contractor Insurance Policies, including the payment of all deductibles and self-insured retention amounts...

relationships.relationship_type: Notification and Replacement Obligation upon Insurer Insolvency or Cancellation
relationships.legal_significance: If an insurer withdraws, cancels, or becomes insolvent, Contractor must promptly notify Company and replace the insurance with an acceptable insurer to maintain coverage.
relationships.evidence_quote: Should any insurer that issues any of the Contractor Insurance Policies withdraw or cancel its policy... (a) Contractor shall promptly give Company written notice thereof, (b) Contractor shall promptly replace such insurance through another insurer acceptable to Company...

relationships.relationship_type: Condition Precedent to Initial Site Mobilization
relationships.legal_significance: Providing evidence of Contractor Insurance Policies to Company is a condition precedent to the Initial Site Mobilization, legally conditioning commencement of Work on insurance compliance.
relationships.evidence_quote: evidence of such coverage shall be provided to Company as a condition precedent to Initial Site Mobilization.

extractor_notes: The clause extensively details insurance obligations of Contractor, Subcontractors, and Company, emphasizing Contractor's duty to maintain insurance, provide evidence, and cooperate in claims. The clause also establishes Company’s rights to verify and replace insurance if Contractor fails, and conditions Initial Site Mobilization on insurance compliance. The waiver of subrogation provisions and the survival of cooperation duties post-termination are notable. The clause explicitly disclaims any limitation on Contractor’s liability despite insurance coverage.

source_clause_title: 17.4 CONTINUING OBLIGATIONS AND RIGHTS. When any Dispute occurs and is the subject of negotiations or litigation, Contractor shall continue the Work in accordance with the Project

entities.name: Contractor

entities.evidence_quote: Contractor shall continue the Work in accordance with the Project Schedule and the terms hereof

entities.name: Dispute
entities.legal_role: A disagreement or conflict between the Parties that triggers the continuing obligations and dispute resolution procedures under Article XVII.
entities.evidence_quote: When any Dispute occurs and is the subject of negotiations or litigation

entities.name: procedures specified in this Article XVII
entities.legal_role: The sole and exclusive procedures agreed by the Parties for resolving Disputes, including tolling statutes of limitations and good faith participation requirements.
entities.evidence_quote: the procedures specified in this Article XVII shall be the sole, exclusive procedures for the resolution of Disputes

entities.name: applicable statutes of limitation and defenses
entities.legal_role: Legal time limits for bringing claims or defenses which are tolled while Article XVII procedures are pending to preserve Parties' rights.

entities.name: preliminary injunction or other preliminary judicial relief
entities.legal_role: An exception allowing a Party to seek urgent court orders outside the exclusive dispute resolution procedures to avoid irreparable damage.

relationships.relationship_type: Obligation to Continue Work During Dispute
relationships.legal_significance: The Contractor must continue performing the Work despite the existence of a Dispute, ensuring project continuity.
relationships.evidence_quote: Contractor shall continue the Work in accordance with the Project Schedule and the terms hereof

relationships.relationship_type: Obligation to Pay Undisputed Amounts During Dispute
relationships.legal_significance: The Company must continue making payments of undisputed amounts during a Dispute, maintaining financial flow under the contract.
relationships.evidence_quote: Company shall continue to make payments of undisputed amounts in accordance with the Contract Documents

relationships.relationship_type: Mandatory Good Faith Participation in Dispute Resolution Procedures
relationships.legal_significance: The Parties are required to continue participating in good faith in the exclusive dispute resolution procedures, ensuring the process is respected and effective.
relationships.evidence_quote: the Parties will continue to participate in good faith in the procedures specified in this Article XVII

relationships.relationship_type: Tolling of Statutes of Limitation During Dispute Resolution
relationships.legal_significance: The statutes of limitation are tolled while dispute resolution procedures are pending, preserving Parties' rights to bring claims or defenses.
relationships.evidence_quote: All applicable statutes of limitation ... shall be tolled while the procedures specified in this Article XVII are pending

relationships.relationship_type: Right to Seek Preliminary Judicial Relief Despite Exclusive Procedures
relationships.legal_significance: Although dispute resolution procedures are exclusive, a Party may seek urgent judicial relief to prevent irreparable harm, balancing exclusivity with emergency protections.
relationships.evidence_quote: any Party may seek a preliminary injunction or other preliminary judicial relief if ... necessary to avoid irreparable damage

extractor_notes: The clause establishes continuing obligations during disputes, tolling of statutes of limitation, exclusivity of dispute resolution procedures, and an exception for preliminary judicial relief to prevent irreparable harm.
